commands.repair_reason: Human maintainer review is required because the PR is protected and changes dependency ownership, executable resolution, and sandbox command-boundary behavior without a narrow automated repair blocker.; Cleared: The diff removes an unintended third-party executable dependency; the sandbox executable and SSH proxy paths are security-sensitive, but I found no concrete code-introduced vulnerability. commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the OpenShell plugin dependency on npm `openshell@0.1.0`, keeps fallback to the `openshell` command, forwards configured gateway endpoints through CLI and SSH proxy paths, sanitizes sandbox names, shares sandbox backend registrations across module instances, and adds focused regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can prefer the package-local `openshell@0.1.0` binary, and the npm tarball shows that binary loads a Telegram bridge config that throws without `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is required because the PR is protected and changes dependency ownership, executable resolution, and sandbox command-boundary behavior without a narrow automated repair blocker.

target.title: Fix OpenShell sandbox backend CLI resolution
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #56813. This removes the accidental dependency on the unrelated npm `openshell` package and keeps the OpenShell sandbox backend pointed at the real standalone OpenShell CLI when no bundled command is available. It also tightens the OpenShell path by: - falling back to `openshell` on `PATH` when no bundled OpenShell command exists - passing configured gateway endpoints through OpenShell CLI and SSH proxy flows - making OpenShell sandbox names Kubernetes-safe - keeping sandbox backend registrations stable across duplicated module loads - adding targeted regression coverage for the OpenShell backend and CLI helpers ## Real Behavior Proof Behavior addressed: OpenClaw's OpenShell sandbox backend no longer resolves the unrelated npm `openshell@0.1.0` Telegram bot package, which required `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` and caused `exec` tool startup failures. Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix-openshell`; focused OpenShell backend tests run through the repo Vitest wrapper. Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openshell/src/backend.test.ts extensions/openshell/src/openshell-core.test.ts` `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` Evidence after fix: The targeted OpenShell backend/CLI regression tests passed, including fallback-to-PATH behavior when no bundled OpenShell command is present. Observed result after fix: The test command exited successfully, and whitespace validation passed. OpenClaw + standalone OpenShell screenshot proof
